texto: Pretend that you're a clever shepherd girl, and you're just dressed up in pretty clothes, and you're trying to make everybody believe that you're a spoiled, empty-headed little princess. So no one guesses that under your clothes you're a brave shepherd girl who climbs trees and chases away wolves with your staff.
explicacao: Citação de Johanna Sinisalo sobre temas ligados a coragem. “Pretend that you're a clever shepherd girl, and you're just dressed up in pretty clothes, and you…” articula coragem, bravery, pretending em registro de coragem, com relevância para contextos como termino e pretending.

frase_original: Pretend that you're a clever shepherd girl, and you're just dressed up in pretty clothes, and you're trying to make everybody believe that you're a spoiled, empty-headed little princess. So no one guesses that under your clothes you're a brave shepherd girl who climbs trees and chases away wolves with your staff.

texto: It is said, once a wise man from the far North told me; it is said that there are in certain parts of Scandinavia cities within cities like there are circles within circles; existent yet invisible. And those cities are inhabited by creatures more terrible than imagination can create : man-shaped but man-devouring, as black and as silent as the night they prowl in.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Johanna Sinisalo: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “It is said, once a wise man from the far North told me; it is said that there are in certain part…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, fantasy, fiction pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.
